title: Tenant Canonical Domain Set Unset Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the tenant canonical domain set unset capability, recovered from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage, not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facet descriptions are concise source-backed summaries citing verified manifest rows; proof is hermetic/stale, not current readiness. Enrich facet detail and publish fresh proof before generation; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: Canonical designation is a guarded set/unset: setting is refused when the domain is not verified, the auth client is inactive, or local routing is not proven, and a successful set flips the canonical flag and the redirect policy to redirect-slug-to-canonical. A second set atomically replaces the prior canonical domain, unset clears the flag, and deactivating the domain also clears canonical.

stateModel.description: State is a canonical boolean plus a canonical timestamp and the redirect policy, constrained so at most one enabled canonical domain exists per tenant. Canonical is a marker only: it does not upgrade routing status or TLS status, which remain governed by their own lifecycle.

permissions.description: Canonical set and unset require the tenant-scoped domain-write permission and operate only on the owning tenant's domains. The single-canonical-per-tenant invariant is enforced within that tenant's scope.

contracts.description: The contract surface is a canonical set operation and a canonical unset operation backed by set/unset canonical use cases, mediated by the tenant-domain port and auth-client adapter. The semantics are expressed abstractly against the port rather than a concrete provider.

validation.description: Setting canonical requires verified ownership, an active auth client, and proven local routing, while unset requires the domain to exist, and a second set preserves the one-canonical invariant by atomically replacing the existing canonical row. These preconditions are checked before any state mutation.

errorModel.description: Outcomes are typed and fail-closed as ok, not-found, not-verified, auth-client-inactive, or routing-not-proven, so each unmet precondition produces a distinct deterministic result rather than a partial change. The replacement of an existing canonical is atomic so the invariant cannot be transiently violated.

auditModel.description: Set and unset emit canonical set and unset audit events carrying domain and redirect-policy metadata before the registry mutation. Audit precedes the state change so every canonical transition is recorded ahead of being applied.

readinessModel.description: Readiness covers local canonical cutover only; public DNS and TLS are explicitly deferred, and canonical is treated as a marker that does not imply public routing or TLS readiness. The capability therefore degrades honestly to a local-only posture when external infrastructure is unavailable.

proof.description: Behaviour is exercised by a canonical-domain runtime proof that validates live registry guards, redirect-policy transition, atomic replacement, unset, and clearing on deactivation against a real substrate. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model before generation.
